findings.title: Most learn command tests assert string presence rather than behavioral safety (quoting, portability, multiline JSON)
findings.impact: High-risk regressions (e.g. command injection, broken mv/cat, evidence quoting) can ship undetected.
findings.recommendation: Add targeted tests for shell-safe arg building + portability (no mv/cat) and add parser tests for multi-line JSON outputs.

findings.title: JSON parsing helpers do not support pretty (multi-line) JSON, while some tools support --pretty output
findings.snippet: Scans lines backwards; returns the last line that JSON.parse() accepts
findings.impact: If any pipeline enables pretty output (e.g. mcp-runner --pretty), command docs using lastJsonObjectFromText can fail.
findings.recommendation: Support multi-line JSON blocks (brace matching) and keep the single-line fast path.

leads.description: Review learn-doc-json-parse-robustness.test.js and extend it to cover multi-line JSON
